title: Multiplication and division word problems
description: Practice solving multiplication and division word problems. Some problems have remainders.

assessment_items.question_content:
Skylar has $4$ times as many books as Karen.

**If Skylar has $164$ books, how many books does Karen have?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $K$ for the number of Karen's books. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $K$ if $4$ groups of $K$ is the same as $164$ books?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$4\times K=164$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$164\div 4 = K$

choices.content: Karen has $656$ books, because $164 \times 4 = 656$.
Karen has $14$ books, because $164 \div 4=14$.
Karen has $41$ books, because $164 \div 4=41$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tannis's first machine could throw a car $27$ meters. She studied her first machine and built a better one. Her second machine could throw a car $7$ times as far.

**How far does the second machine throw a car?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $d$ for the distance the second machine can throw the car. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

How far is $d$ if $d$ is $7$ times as far as $27$ meters?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$27\times 7=d$

[[☃ image 1]]

The machine throws the car $189$ meters, because $27 \times 7 =189$.

choices.content: The machine throws the car $189$ meters, because $27 \times 7 =189$.
The machine throws the car $3$ meters, because $27 \div 7 =3$ remainder $6$.
The machine throws the car $4$ meters, because $27 \div 7 =3$ remainder $6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jenna's dogs used $9$ chew toys last year. The animal shelter used $2{,}277$ chew toys. 

**How many times as many chew toys did the animal shelter use?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: $\text{chew toys animal shelter used}={?} \times \text{chew toys Jenna's dogs used}$

We can fill in the given information.

$2{,}277 = {?} \times 9$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$2{,}277 \div 9 = {?}$
The animal shelter used $253$ times as many chew toys, because $2{,}277 \div 9 = 253$.

choices.content: The animal shelter used $20{,}493$ times as many chew toys, because $9 \times 2{,}277 = 20{,}493$.
The animal shelter used $2{,}053$ times as many chew toys, because $2{,}277 \div 9 = 2{,}053$.
The animal shelter used $253$ times as many chew toys, because $2{,}277 \div 9 = 253$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leslie tunes musical instruments. Leslie tuned a guitar with $6$ strings today. Then she tuned a piano with $37$ times as many strings.

**How many strings did the piano have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $p$ for the number of strings on the piano. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $p$ if $p$ is $37$ times as many as $6$ strings?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$6\times 37=p$

[[☃ image 1]]

The piano has $222$ strings, because $37 \times 6=222$.

choices.content: The piano has $422$ strings, because $37 \times 6=422$.
The piano has $222$ strings, because $37 \times 6=222$.
The piano has $6$ strings, because $37 \div 6=6$ remainder $1$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A seal has $1{,}500$ nerves per whisker. That is $10$ times as many as a rat has.

**How many nerves per whisker does a rat have?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $r$ for the number of nerves per rat whisker. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $r$ if $10$ times as many as $r$ is equal to $1{,}500$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$10\times r=1{,}500$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$1{,}500\div 10 = r$

When **dividing** by $10$, we shift our numbers $1$ place value to the right.

Number | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$1{,}500$ |$\searrow\goldD{1}$ | $\searrow\purpleD{5}$ | $\searrow\blueD{0}$ | $\searrow\greenD{0}$  
$1{,}500 \div 10$ |$?$ |$?$ |$?$| $?$

[[☃ image 1]]

A rat has $150$ nerves per whisker because $1{,}500 \div 10 = 150$.

choices.content: A rat has $15{,}000$ nerves per whisker because $10 \times 1{,}500 = 15{,}000$.
A rat has $150$ nerves per whisker because $1{,}500 \div 10 = 150$.
A rat has $1{,}510$ nerves per whisker because $1{,}500 \times 10 = 1{,}510$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Quina is cooking fish for a group of travelers. Quina has $78$ huge fish, and each fish can feed $3$ travelers.

**How many travelers can Quina feed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $t$ for the total number of travelers. Then we can fill in given information.

$78\times 3=t$

choices.content: Quina can feed $26$ travelers, because $78\div 3=26$.
Quina can feed $22$ travelers, because $78\div 3=22$.
Quina can feed $234$ travelers, because $78\times 3=234$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ares is making $14$ jars of honey peanut butter. He wants to use $45$ milliliters $(\text{mL})$ of honey in each jar.

**How much honey (in $\text{mL}$) will Ares use in all?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $h$ for the total amount of honey (in $\text{mL}$). We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $h$ if $14$ groups of $45$ is the same as $h$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$14\times 45=h$

[[☃ image 1]]

Ares uses $630\,\text{mL}$ of honey, because $14\times 45=630$.

choices.content: Ares uses $630\,\text{mL}$ of honey, because $14\times 45=630$.
Ares uses $1\,\text{mL}$ of honey, because $14 \div 45=0$, remainder $14$.
Ares uses $3\,\text{mL}$ of honey, because $45\div 14=3$, remainder $3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kira unravels a shirt to get blue thread. From each centimeter $(\text{cm})$ of the shirt, Kira gets $231\,\text{cm}$ of thread. She unravels $7\,\text{cm}$ of the shirt.

**How much thread does Kira get?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $\ell$ for the length of thread Kira gets. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

How long is $\ell$ if $\ell$ is $7$ times as long as $231\,\text{cm}$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$7\times 231=\ell$

[[☃ image 1]]

Kira gets $1{,}617\,\text{cm}$ of thread, because $7 \times 231 = 1{,}617$.

choices.content: Kira gets $1{,}617\,\text{cm}$ of thread, because $7 \times 231 = 1{,}617$.
Kira gets $33\,\text{cm}$ of thread, because $7 \times 33 = 231$.
Kira gets $30\,\text{cm}$ of thread, because $231 \div 7= 30$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amanda is mailing $5$ remote control cars. The mass has a total of $3{,}470$ grams $(\text{g})$.

**What is the mass of each car?**  
(Assume each car has the same mass.)  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $m$ for the mass per car. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $m$ if $5$ groups of $m$ is the same as $3{,}470$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$5 \times m = 3{,}470$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rrrl}
&m\\
{5}&\overline{\Big)3{,}470}\\
\end{array}$

[[☃ image 1]]

Each car has a mass of $694\,\text{g}$, because $3{,}470\div5=694$.

choices.content: Each car has a mass of $17{,}350\,\text{g}$ because $3{,}470\times 5=17{,}350$.
Each car has a mass of $694\,\text{g}$, because $3{,}470\div5=694$.
Each car has a mass of $1{,}735\,\text{g}$ because $3{,}470\times 5=1{,}735$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jenna has $131$ dog biscuits. She wants to give them as gifts to each of her $4$ poodles.

**How many biscuits does each poodle get?**  
(Assume each gets the same number of biscuits.)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $p$ for the number of biscuits per poodle. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]




What is the value of $p$ if $4$ groups of $p$ is the same as $131$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$4 \times p = 131$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rrrl}
&p\\
{4}&\overline{\Big)131}\\
\end{array}$
Since each poodle gets the same number of biscuits, none of them get the $\goldE{3}$ leftover biscuits.

[[☃ image 1]]



Each poodle gets $32$ biscuits because $131 \div 4 = 32$ remainder $3$.

choices.content: Each poodle gets $524$ biscuits because $131 \times 4 = 524$.
Each poodle gets $32$ biscuits because $131 \div 4 = 32$ remainder $3$.
Each poodle gets $33$ biscuits because $131 \div 4 = 32$ remainder $3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Gregor Mendel is examining peas to try to understand how traits are passed from parents to offspring. Today, Gregor has $228$ peas to examine. The pods have $6$ peas per pod.

**How many pods of peas are there?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use $p$ for the number of pods. Then we can fill in given information.

$p \times 6 = 228$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rrrl}
&p\\
{6}&\overline{\Big)228}\\
\end{array}$

choices.content: There are $38$ pods, because $228\div 6= 38$.
There are $1{,}368$ pods, because $228 \times 6= 1{,}368$.
There are $48$ pods, because $228\div 6= 48$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ron and Hermione are lifting feathers into the air with magic. Hermione can lift a feather $3{,}525$ centimeters $(\text{cm})$ into the air. That is $5$ times as high as Ron can lift a feather.

**How high can Ron lift a feather with his magic?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
Let's use $R$ for the distance Ron can lift a feather. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

How far is $R\,\text{cm}$ if $5$ times as far as $R$ is the same as $3{,}525\,\text{cm}$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$5\times R=3{,}525$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$3{,}525\div 5 = R$

[[☃ image 1]]

Ron can lift a feather $\greenE{705}\,\text{cm}$ with his magic.

assessment_items.question_content:
Varian rides a horse. Gelbin rides a rocket-powered robo-ostrich.  Gelbin's ostrich ran $4{,}828$ meters $(\text{m})$, which is $2$ times as far as Varian's horse.

**How far did Varian's horse run?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints:
Let's use $h$ for the distance the horse ran. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $h$ meters if $2$ times as far as $h$ is the same as $4{,}828$ meters?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$2\times h=4{,}828$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$4{,}828\div 2 = h$

assessment_items.question_content:
Frederic read $471$ pages of books this month. Jeane read $3$ times as many pages as Frederic.

**How many pages did Jeane read?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] pages
hints:
Let's use $J$ for the number of pages Jeane read. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $J$ if $J$ is $3$ times as many as $471$ pages?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$471 \times 3 = J$

assessment_items.question_content:
A cat has $24$ whiskers by its nose. A manatee has $25$ times as many whiskers.

**How many whiskers does a manatee have?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] whiskers
hints:
Let's use $m$ for the number of whiskers on a manatee. Then we can fill in the given information.

$m = 25 \times 24$

assessment_items.question_content:
Bry blew $91$ bubbles. Ken blew $7$ bubbles.

**How many times as many bubbles did Bry blow?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times as many bubbles
hints:
We can fill in the given information.

$91 = {?} \times 7$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rr}
&{?}\\
{7}&\overline{\Big)91}\\
\end{array}$
Bry blew $13$ times as many bubbles.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lily is organizing a large team of volunteers to help with a community project. Each group of volunteers consists of $9$ people.

**How many groups does Lily need to organize if there are $6{,}174$ volunteers?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] groups
hints:
Let's use $g$ for the number of groups. Then we can fill in given information.

$g \times 9 = 6{,}174$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rrrl}
&g\\
{9}&\overline{\Big)6{,}174}\\
\end{array}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Legolas shoots $3$ arrows at once from his bow. He has $177$ arrows.

**How many times can Legolas shoot his bow before he needs more arrows?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] times
hints:
Let's use $t$ for the number of times he shoots his bow. Then we can fill in given information.

$t \times 3 = 177$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rrrl}
&t\\
{3}&\overline{\Big)177}\\
\end{array}$
Legolas can shoot his bow $59$ times.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nissa is going to plant $485$ trees this year. 

**If Nissa plants $5$ orchards of trees, how many trees will be in each orchard?**  
(Assume each orchard has the same number of trees.)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] trees
hints:
Let's use $t$ for the number of trees per orchard. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $t$ if $5$ groups of $t$ is the same as $485$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$5 \times t = 485$

We can rewrite the relationship with division.

$\begin{array}{rrrl}
&t\\
{5}&\overline{\Big)485}\\
\end{array}$

[[☃ image 1]]

There will be $97$ trees in each orchard.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leia is setting up $417$ toy cars. Each car needs $3$ batteries to drive.

**How many batteries does Leia need for her cars?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] batteries
hints:
Let's use $b$ for the total number of batteries. Then we can fill in given information.

$417\times 3=b$
Leia needs $1{,}251$ batteries for her cars.

assessment_items.question_content:
Zane is putting the strings on $5$ harps. Each harp has $48$ strings.

**How many strings does Zane need for all $5$ harps?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] strings
hints:
Let's use $t$ for the total number of strings. We can make a model to show the given information.

[[☃ image 1]]

What is the value of $t$ if $5$ groups of $48$ is the same as $t$?

We can write this as a multiplication problem.

$5\times 48=t$
